title: Measuring ROS production by neutrophils using DCFH-DA probe and flow Cytometry
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fis5a","text":"Neutrophils are isolated, stained with the DCFH-DA probe, and incubated with or without stimuli (e.g., PMA or fMLP) to induce ROS production. After incubation, fluorescence is measured by flow cytometry to assess intracellular ROS levels. Stimulated neutrophils should exhibit increased fluorescence intensity compared to unstimulated controls, indicating higher ROS production. The presence of inhibitors like DPI should result in a significant reduction in fluorescence, confirming the specificity of ROS detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"aajb3","text":"To ensure the accuracy, reproducibility, and safety of the experiment, the following guidelines should be followed:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"chsrp","text":"Use anticoagulants like EDTA or heparin to prevent clotting.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9sv0l","text":"Process blood samples promptly (within 2–4 hours) to ensure neutrophil viability.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7go9u","text":"Ensure \u003e95% cell viability using trypan blue or propidium iodide staining before proceeding with the assay.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b4o19","text":"Handle cells gently to avoid activation or damage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2v2p7","text":"Always include appropriate controls (negative control, positive control with PMA, and inhibitor control with DPI) to validate the experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"m9d","text":"Use biological replicates (at least 3) to ensure statistical significance.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"16dm8","text":"Maintain consistent cell concentrations across samples (e.g., 1 × 10⁶ cells/mL).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d21d0","text":"Use freshly prepared reagents to prevent degradation and variability.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q6dt","text":"Prepare DCFH-DA solutions under low light conditions to prevent premature oxidation.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"etvgr","text":"Aliquot reagents to avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"202om","text":"Set appropriate forward and side scatter parameters to properly gate neutrophil populations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fuv3b","text":"Conduct experiments under identical conditions, such as temperature, incubation times, and reagent batches.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6pf7","text":"Use standardized protocols for sample preparation and data analysis.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1hgs8","text":"Work in a sterile environment to prevent bacterial contamination, which may alter results.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9q3m9","text":"Regularly clean flow cytometry fluidics to avoid cross-contamination.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"379ei","text":"Before starting the experiment, ensure the following conditions are met:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mhob","text":"Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): Wear gloves, lab coat, and safety goggles when handling blood samples and reagents.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":36}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7es1i","text":"Biosafety: Follow biosafety level 2 (BSL-2) guidelines if working with human blood or primary cells.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c3a45","text":"Instrument Calibration: Verify that the flow cytometer is properly calibrated and compensated to avoid false-positive signals.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":23}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"558ch","text":"Reagent Preparation: Prepare fresh DCFH-DA working solutions to prevent auto-oxidation and ensure accurate results.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"enhqo","text":"Sample Collection Ethics: Ensure all human samples are collected with informed consent and under the approval of an Institutional Ethics Committee.","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"76l9f","text":"Biological Hazards: Handle blood-derived neutrophils with care to avoid potential infections. Disinfect work surfaces before and after the experiment.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6v074","text":"Chemical Hazards: PMA and DPI are potentially hazardous; handle them inside a fume hood and dispose of them properly.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"89lud","text":"Laser Safety: Avoid direct exposure to the laser beams of the flow cytometer to prevent eye damage.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"56ume","text":"Neutrophils suspension (isolated from human peripheral blood or animal models)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fk52i","text":"DCFH-DA (2',7'-dichlorodihydrofluorescein diacetate) probe","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1vai","text":"Phosphate-buffered saline (PBS), pH 7.4","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9m3a4","text":"RPMI 1640 medium (with 10% fetal bovine serum, optional)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mpfu","text":"Stimuli for positive control: Phorbol 12-myristate 13-acetate (PMA)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"23q3l","text":"Inhibitors (if needed): Diphenyleneiodonium (DPI, NADPH oxidase inhibitor)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6iav1","text":"Flow cytometer with excitation/emission filters for FITC (488/530 nm)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"pcbd","text":"FACS tubes or 96-well plates","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"v09g","text":"Refrigerated centrifuge and 37°C water bath","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Isolate neutrophils from whole blood using density gradient centrifugation (e.g., Ficoll-Paque).
Wash cells twice with PBS to remove any residual isolation medium.
Count the cells and adjust the concentration to approximately 1 × 10⁶ cells/mL in RPMI 1640 or PBS.
Prepare the DCFH-DA working solution at a final concentration of 5–10 µM in PBS.
Add the DCFH-DA to the neutrophil suspension and incubate for 20–30 minutes at 37°C in the dark , allowing the probe to penetrate the cells and be hydrolyzed to non-fluorescent DCFH.
After incubation, wash cells with PBS to remove excess probe.

Divide cells into different experimental groups , such as: Unstimulated control (no treatment) Positive control (treated with 100 nM PMA) Experimental groups (treated with stimuli of interest, e.g., fMLP) Inhibitor groups (pretreated with DPI before stimulation)
Incubate cells with the desired stimulus for 30–60 minutes at 37°C .
After incubation, immediately place cells on ice to stop the reaction
Analyze fluorescence using a flow cytometer, with excitation at 488 nm and emission at 530 nm (FITC channel).
Set up the flow cytometer using unstained cells (gate) to establish background fluorescence.
Acquire at least 10,000 events per sample.
Analyze data in terms of: Mean fluorescence intensity (MFI) , which reflects the level of ROS production. Percentage of positive cells producing ROS.
Compare results across conditions to determine changes in ROS levels.
Increased fluorescence indicates higher ROS production. Compare MFI values between treated and control samples. Use statistical tests (e.g., ANOVA or t-test) to assess significance.
creator: Valeria Duarte de Almeida
